topic: Unified angular coordinate theta = -lonE — post EXP-187 consistency-test-defense preemption review
change_summary: EXP-187 integrated: (a) extended finding with one-sentence preemption of the model-consistency-test reframing defense; (b) inserted a 2,516-char "Preempting the consistency-test defense" block into detail_evidence before the Registry-integrity-notes block, invoking Snyder (1987) polar AE projection, an architectural-axiom framing, and the Paris-meridian circularity demonstration. Decider batch 729ad70 (2026-04-20T04:35Z) applied the five prior-cycle c4 minor patches (176° vs 183° standardization, concession→concessions plural fix, At-least-three closer hedge, finding 600–730× widening, finding more precise 0.9941 framing). This c5 cycle adversarially re-examines the new preemption content and reports residual holes.
our_argument_summary: The EXP-187 preemption block credits the charitable model-consistency-test reframing of WIN-058 (theta = -lonE discriminates polar-azimuthal projections from non-polar ones), then collapses the defense on two structural grounds: (i) the dome polar-centered disc geometry is an architectural axiom committed before V13, so the test compares the dome only to projection classes its own geometry had already excluded; (ii) any globe-based polar azimuthal equidistant projection satisfies theta = -lonE identically (Snyder 1987 ch. 25), so the identity cannot discriminate flat from spherical geometry — only polar from non-polar. The finding closes with the same argument in one sentence. The preemption content is structurally sound and the verdict Misleading holds. Residual issues found in this review are copy-editing (curly quotes, terminology drift, internal number mismatch) and a minor overclaim (tautology vs definitional identity, where the latter is more precise).

relevant_files: raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (theta = delta_lon × 0.9941 live in V12 per dome own coordinates page, line 14)
data/wins.json WIN-057 (two-zone topology — cross-referenced in stronger_arguments)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change from c4. The V13 identity theta = -lonE is definitional; no derivation from dome parameters enters. V12 0.9941 EW scale still applied to angular differences in distance computation (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt lines 14-16: theta = delta_lon × 0.9941 (EW angular scale); delta_theta = delta_lon × 0.9941).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No runtime check possible (definitional identity). monitor.py has no theta check. This is correctly tagged monitoring=none.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-001 (WGS-84 dependency); OPEN-003 (0.9941 interpretation); OPEN-015 (near-equatorial exclusion).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: The raw-text/04-coordinates.txt confirms theta = delta_lon × 0.9941 and the coexistence with V13 theta = -lonE. The prior-cycle unchecked citation (delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941 in distance_analysis.py) is not directly visible in raw-text/ but the behavioral equivalent (delta_theta = delta_lon × 0.9941) is present and confirms the substantive point that the V13 identity and V12 scale factor coexist. No new discrepancies introduced by EXP-187.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain or API call for a theta = -lonE check. The identity is a coordinate convention, not a measurable quantity. Confirmed by reading raw-text/monitor.py (no theta-fetch) and raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (theta defined by formula, not by observation).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: theta = -lonE is the centuries-old east-longitude sign-flip convention used by any polar-azimuthal projection. The Snyder (1987) ch. 25 citation added by EXP-187 is correct — AE polar aspect has been standard cartography since the 19th century.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Geographic east longitude was defined long before the dome model existed (Greenwich meridian adopted 1884; prior conventions earlier). The dome CONFIRMED 2026-03-28 badge is definitional adoption, not prospective test.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula traces from disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086, or any dome-specific parameter to theta = -lonE. The chain solar_noon_UTC ~ 12 - lonE/15h then theta = (noon_UTC - 12) × 15° = -lonE uses only standard longitude-to-solar-time conversion. The EXP-187 preemption block correctly identifies this as architectural-axiom unpacking, not prediction.

holes_found.description: Residual 600-700× vs 600-730× inconsistency inside detail_evidence. The decider batch 729ad70 (2026-04-20T04:35Z, patch WIN-058 H5) widened the finding range from 600-700× to 600-730×, but the matching sentence inside detail_evidence was NOT updated. Current detail_evidence still reads: "The dome coordinate system, even after the V13 fix, is 600-700× less accurate than the system it takes its inputs from (best-case: 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE vs sub-0.01% gives ~620×; worst-case: 7.3% NH mean error gives ~730×)." The outer range (600-700×) now contradicts its own immediately-following parenthetical (worst-case ~730×), and it also contradicts the finding (600-730×). A defender reading carefully can point at this as either (a) sloppy arithmetic (our parenthetical exceeds our range) or (b) inconsistency between summary and detail. The c4 recommendation explicitly noted this fix needed to happen in both places — finding got patched, detail_evidence did not.
holes_found.recommendation: In WIN-058 detail_evidence, change "is 600-700× less accurate" to "is 600-730× less accurate" so the outer range matches both (i) the parenthetical that follows it and (ii) the finding field. Mechanical one-character-range edit; low risk.

holes_found.description: Terminology drift within the new finding preemption sentence. The sentence reads: "Even read charitably as a model-consistency test, the identity distinguishes only the polar-azimuthal projection class — a family the dome was already committed to by its disc-centered geometry — so it cannot discriminate between the dome and any globe-based polar-centered projection using the same WGS-84 inputs." Three distinct descriptors for the same geometric class appear in a single sentence: polar-azimuthal projection class, disc-centered geometry, and polar-centered projection. These are adjacent but not identical concepts: (a) polar-azimuthal specifies a projection family (azimuths from the pole preserved); (b) disc-centered is ambiguous — a disc can in principle be centered elsewhere; (c) polar-centered is broader than polar-azimuthal (any projection centered on the pole, not necessarily azimuthal). A defender can exploit the slippage: the class the dome belongs to is polar-azimuthal; but you say the identity fails to discriminate against any polar-centered projection — a broader class — so the argument overreaches. The argument is correct in substance (any polar-azimuthal projection satisfies theta = -lonE) but the sentence elides precision.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten terminology to one term throughout the finding sentence. Recommend settling on polar-azimuthal as primary since it is the technical term that captures the constraint-satisfying class. Suggested rewrite: "Even read charitably as a model-consistency test, the identity distinguishes only the polar-azimuthal projection class — a family the dome was already committed to by its polar-centered disc geometry — so it cannot discriminate between the dome and any globe-based polar-azimuthal projection using the same WGS-84 inputs." (Replace third polar-centered with polar-azimuthal to match the first; retain polar-centered as modifier of disc geometry since the disc is polar-centered by architectural choice.)

holes_found.description: The EXP-187 preemption block substantially duplicates the argument already made in the earlier "Why this is not a prediction" paragraph. The earlier paragraph says: "It is true on any map projection centered on the North Pole, not specifically on a flat disc." The preemption block restates the same point: "any globe-based polar azimuthal equidistant projection ... satisfies theta = -lonE identically ... the identity discriminates only polar from non-polar projections." The preemption does add the structural framing (architectural axiom, Paris meridian demo, Snyder citation), but the core takeaway for a reader is the same. At 2,516 chars, the preemption block is now the longest sub-block in detail_evidence (the total is 10,655 chars). A reader who already absorbed "this is a coordinate convention, not dome physics" from the earlier paragraph may skim the preemption and miss the new defense-specific framing. Not a correctness issue — a readability and bloat concern.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider a future consolidation pass (probably a separate EXP): merge the "Why this is not a prediction" paragraph and the "Preempting the consistency-test defense" block into a single structured section with sub-headings (e.g., "The identity is not derived from dome physics" and "Nor does it discriminate polar-azimuthal projections"). Current state is correct but verbose. Not blocking; low priority. Alternatively, if the preemption block is intended to stand alone for defenders, tighten it by dropping the "what the defense misses" sentences that restate earlier paragraphs.

holes_found.description: The preemption block uses HTML curly-quote entities (“ / ”) for the reframed defense quote ("does the V13 coordinate system recover the correct angles in both hemispheres with a single parameterless equation?"). The rest of the detail_evidence uses straight single quotes (confirmed prediction, EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation, falls beyond the equatorial ring). The mixed quote styles render inconsistently in the final HTML: one instance uses typographic quotes, all other quoted-string instances use ASCII apostrophes. Cosmetic but noticeable in rendered output, and a defender scanning for stylistic tells will find it.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace “ ... ” in the preemption block with the same ASCII single-quote pattern used elsewhere in detail_evidence: change “does the V13... to does the V13... and the matching closing quote. Mechanical search-and-replace; zero risk.

holes_found.description: The preemption block asserts the polar-centered disc geometry is an architectural axiom of the model without citing where the dome establishes this. A more rigorous version would point to raw-text/05-model.txt (or whichever dome page establishes the disc-centered-on-North-Pole architecture) to make the axiom claim verifiable. Without a citation, a pedantic defender could reply axiom according to whom — the answer is obvious if you know the model, but the block does not walk the reader to the receipt. Not load-bearing (the claim is uncontroversial to anyone familiar with the dome), but citations are cheap insurance.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical or footnote linking to the dome model architecture page (raw-text/05-model.txt has the disc parameters and polar-centered setup; dome site equivalent is docs/model.html on john09289.github.io). Suggested inline insertion: the North-Pole-centered disc geometry is an architectural axiom of the model (established in the dome model.html with disc_radius=20015 km centered on the geographic North Pole), not an outcome of any coordinate test. Alternatively, add as a closing citation footnote. Lowest priority.

holes_found.description: Overclaim risk: the preemption block calls theta = -lonE a tautology about the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy in one sentence and a definitional identity inside the projection family the dome had already chosen in the next sentence. These are not identical: a TAUTOLOGY is true by the meanings of its terms alone (a proposition-level property); a DEFINITIONAL IDENTITY is true because one of the terms is defined in terms of the other (a term-level property). theta = -lonE is more cleanly the latter — it is true because the dome has DEFINED theta as the angle west of Greenwich, which by convention is -lonE. Calling it a tautology invites a philosophically sharp defender to reply: no, it is just a choice of definition; changing the definition changes the statement — a tautology truth is preserved under all reinterpretations. The critique still lands, but definitional identity is the more defensible framing. The Paris-meridian demonstration in the preceding sentence actually supports definitional identity (shift the prime meridian, the definition of lonE shifts, and theta still tracks it), not strict tautology.
holes_found.recommendation: In the preemption block, change a tautology about the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy to a definitional identity that follows from the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy. This aligns the two adjacent sentences on the same technical term (definitional identity) and removes the weaker tautology claim without weakening the substantive critique. One-phrase edit.

stronger_arguments.description: Push the constraint-chain counter-defense preemptively. The current preemption block addresses the consistency-test framing but leaves open a related defender move: theta = -lonE is one relation in a constraint chain — combine it with WIN-057 (two-zone topology r_SH = 2 × 14,105 - r_NH) and other coordinate-layer WINs and the chain collectively is discriminating even if each link individually is weak. A defender who has read our review will naturally escalate from the single-WIN consistency test to a multi-WIN constraint chain. The preemption block does not address this. One possible framing: A fallback reframing concedes WIN-058 individually is not discriminating and pivots to a multi-WIN constraint chain (theta = -lonE + two-zone topology + r_eq = 14,105 km + ...). This fails for the same axiom-unpacking reason scaled up: each link in the chain is either derived from dome architectural axioms or from WGS-84 inputs, so the chain does not cumulate predictive content — it cumulates constraints on the projection family, a class the dome had already chosen.
sources: WIN-057 (two-zone topology) cross-reference already listed in EXP-187; Snyder 1987 general polar projection family — any chain of polar-projection-preserving relations carries the same circularity as any single one.

stronger_arguments.description: Sharpen the architectural-axiom argument by citing the dome own admission. The dome OPEN-001 ("Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 — V12 uses longitude × 0.9941 as proxy") is effectively an admission that the dome has no independent coordinate system — it depends on WGS-84. This is a stronger version of the architectural-axiom argument: the dome has conceded it has no alternative to the WGS-84-based polar-azimuthal projection. Currently detail_evidence mentions OPEN-001 but does not link it to the preemption architectural-axiom framing. One extra sentence in the preemption block would tie the two together: The dome own OPEN-001 concedes there is no dome-native alternative to WGS-84 longitude — i.e., the polar-azimuthal projection on the WGS-84 datum is not a choice but the only available geometry. Passing a consistency check against the only geometry you have is not discriminating.
sources: Dome OPEN-001 (cited elsewhere in detail_evidence — Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 — V12 uses longitude × 0.9941 as proxy).

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-187 correctly identifies and credits the strongest defense available: a charitable reader CAN reframe WIN-058 as a model-consistency test, and the identity theta = -lonE does narrowly discriminate polar-azimuthal from non-polar projection families. The Snyder (1987) citation is accurate (USGS PP 1395 ch. 25 covers polar AE). The architectural-axiom argument is structurally correct: the dome polar-centered disc IS committed in the model Part 1 geometry before any coordinate test, so a test against non-polar projections tests nothing the dome could have failed.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Finding the strongest defense and then dismantling it is good adversarial practice — the preemption block is doing the right thing. The residual issues in this c5 review are copy-editing (terminology drift, curly quotes, residual 600-700× number) and one sharper technical critique (tautology vs definitional identity) that does not change the verdict. The Misleading verdict is correctly preserved because the CONFIRMED 2026-03-28 badge on the live dome site asserts a successful predictive test that the coordinate identity cannot be — and the registry-integrity-notes acknowledgement (2026-04-17) lives only at the machine-readable layer. None of the c5 residuals weaken the case. The only non-copy-edit item (stronger_argument #1 — constraint-chain preemption) is a defensive fortification, not a required fix.

deeper_analysis: The EXP-187 preemption is substantively correct and the verdict Misleading holds. The consistency-test defense is the strongest charitable reframing of WIN-058, and the preemption block credits it properly (kernel of truth: V9 to V12 to V13 is real engineering progress; the identity does narrowly discriminate polar-azimuthal from non-polar projections) before collapsing it on two structural grounds: (i) the dome polar-centered disc geometry is committed by architectural axiom before V13, so the test compares against projection classes the dome geometry excluded; (ii) any globe-based polar-AE projection satisfies theta = -lonE identically using the same WGS-84 inputs (Snyder 1987 ch. 25), so the identity cannot discriminate flat-vs-curved — only polar-vs-non-polar. Advocate defense_survives drops from 3 (curmudgeon c3 rating) to 1-2 after EXP-187; the remaining 1-2 is the residual that hostile defenders will fall back to the constraint-chain reframing (stronger_arguments #1). Six holes found in c5: two moderate (600-700 vs 600-730 residual inconsistency, terminology drift in finding), four minor (preemption bloat/duplication, curly-quote inconsistency, missing axiom citation, tautology-vs-definitional-identity overclaim). None verdict-changing. Recommended_action: minor_edit — all six findings are mechanical text fixes. If the decider wants to batch, the two moderate items are worth prioritizing (they can be used by a defender); the four minors are housekeeping.

citations_verified: Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, USGS Professional Paper 1395, ch. 25 — confirmed this covers Azimuthal Equidistant Projection (standard geographic cartography reference, polar aspect in ch. 25)
Dome theta = delta_lon × 0.9941 (V12 live formula) — verified in raw-text/04-coordinates.txt lines 14-16 and line 83
Dome V13 formula theta = -lonE — verified consistent with raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 27 (theta = (solar_noon_utc - 12.0) × 15 deg, which resolves to -lonE when solar_noon_UTC ~ 12 - lonE/15h)
Sydney longitude 151.2 east — standard geography (longitude of Sydney CBD is approximately 151.2 east, consistent with the quoted value)
citations_unchecked: distance_analysis.py delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941 — the exact line is still not in raw-text/ (which has coordinates.txt with the equivalent formula but not the Python source). The substantive claim (V12 0.9941 factor coexists with V13 theta identity) is confirmed by raw-text/04-coordinates.txt, so even if the specific Python line is paraphrased, the argument holds. Lower-priority than c4 flagged.

Paris meridian convention pre-1884 — historically accurate (France used the Paris meridian until the 1884 Washington Meridian Conference adopted Greenwich), but the preemption block does not provide a citation. Uncontroversial history; leave unchecked.
